key_insight: PNOT reconstructs divertor temperature fields by combining graph-based boundary physics, physics-aware attention, and gradient-consistent learning to achieve fast, physically coherent predictions.
engineering_relevance: This enables near real-time temperature-field estimation for tungsten monoblock divertors on EAST, supporting safer operation, melting avoidance, and control of fusion heat loads.
method: Models boundary heat-flux relations as a structured graph.
Uses graph attention to capture spatial physical dependencies.
Adds a physics-aware neural operator with slicing-based aggregation plus Sobolev regularization for derivative consistency.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental results showing that the added physics constraints improve prediction accuracy while preserving physical consistency.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of boundary heat-flux graph construction and the availability of representative training data for generalization.
paper_title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
